devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । बलेः पदत्रयं भूमेः कस्माद्धरिरयाचत । भूतेश्वरः कृपणवल्लब्धार्थोऽपि बबन्ध तम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovācabaleḥ padatrayaṃ bhūmeḥ kasmāddharirayācatabhūteśvaraḥ kṛpaṇavallabdhārtho'pi babandha tam
translation:
The King Parīkṣit said:
For what purpose did Lord Hari, though himself the Supreme Ruler, beg like a miserable fellow, three paces of land from Bali? Why did he bind him down even though he got his object sought for?

devanagari: एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामो महत्कौतूहलं हि नः । यज्ञेश्वरस्य पूर्णस्य बन्धनं चाप्यनागसः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: etadveditumicchāmo mahatkautūhalaṃ hi naḥyajñeśvarasya pūrṇasya bandhanaṃ cāpyanāgasaḥ
translation: This we desire to know thoroughly as it is highly surprising to us that the Lord of sacrifices, (though) Perfect (in all respects within himself), begged like a destitute and (subsequently) bound down (with fetters) the innocent (donor).

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । पराजितश्रीरसुभिश्च हापितो हीन्द्रेण राजन्भृगुभिः स जीवितः । सर्वात्मना तानभजद्भृगून्बलिः शिष्यो महात्मार्थनिवेदनेन ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaparājitaśrīrasubhiśca hāpito hīndreṇa rājanbhṛgubhiḥ sa jīvitaḥsarvātmanā tānabhajadbhṛgūnbaliḥ śiṣyo mahātmārthanivedanena
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Being deprived of his splendour, wealth as well as his life through being defeated by Indra in a battle, Bali was revived by Śukra and other members of the Bhṛgu clan, Oh king. The noble-minded Bali, with utmost faith, resorted to the Bhṛgus as a disciple, offering them whatever they desired.

devanagari: तं ब्राह्मणा भृगवः प्रीयमाणा अयाजयन्विश्वजिता त्रिणाकम् । जिगीषमाणं विधिनाभिषिच्य महाभिषेकेण महानुभावाः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ brāhmaṇā bhṛgavaḥ prīyamāṇā ayājayanviśvajitā triṇākamjigīṣamāṇaṃ vidhinābhiṣicya mahābhiṣekeṇa mahānubhāvāḥ
translation: The Brāhmaṇas of the Bhṛgu clan, endowed as they were with extra-ordinary powers and were pleased with him, consecrated him with the great sacred ablution (called aindra—Pertaining to Indra) with due formalities, (as prescribed in the Bahvṛc Brāhmaṇa), and desirous as he was of conquering the celestial region, made him perform the Viśvajit sacrifice.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
This order of ‘consecration’ (abhiṣeka) and performance of sacrifice is given by G.S. In the Viśvajit sacrifice the sacrificer has to give all wealth (after separating the share of the eldest son and land).—Kane’s History of Dharma Śāstra II. ii. 1212, Fn. 2644.

devanagari: ततो रथः काञ्चनपट्टनद्धो हयाश्च हर्यश्वतुरङ्गवर्णाः । ध्वजश्च सिंहेन विराजमानो हुताशनादास हविर्भिरिष्टात् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tato rathaḥ kāñcanapaṭṭanaddho hayāśca haryaśvaturaṅgavarṇāḥdhvajaśca siṃhena virājamāno hutāśanādāsa havirbhiriṣṭāt
translation: From the sacrificial fire duly worshipped with oblations, there emerged a chariot strengthened with gold plates, horses of the same colour (green) as those of Indra, a banner blazing the emblem of a lion, a celestial bow fastened with plates of gold, a pair of inexhaustible quivers full of arrows, a divine coat of mail. His grandfather (Prahlāda) conferred on him a wreath of never-fading flowers and Śukra (his preceptor), a conch.

devanagari: धनुश्च दिव्यं पुरटोपनद्धं तूणावरिक्तौ कवचं च दिव्यम् । पितामहस्तस्य ददौ च मालामम्लानपुष्पां जलजं च शुक्रः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: dhanuśca divyaṃ puraṭopanaddhaṃ tūṇāvariktau kavacaṃ ca divyampitāmahastasya dadau ca mālāmamlānapuṣpāṃ jalajaṃ ca śukraḥ
translation: From the sacrificial fire duly worshipped with oblations, there emerged a chariot strengthened with gold plates, horses of the same colour (green) as those of Indra, a banner blazing the emblem of a lion, a celestial bow fastened with plates of gold, a pair of inexhaustible quivers full of arrows, a divine coat of mail. His grandfather (Prahlāda) conferred on him a wreath of never-fading flowers and Śukra (his preceptor), a conch.

devanagari: एवं स विप्रार्जितयोधनार्थस्तैः कल्पितस्वस्त्ययनोऽथ विप्रान् । प्रदक्षिणीकृत्य कृतप्रणामः प्रह्रादमामन्त्र्य नमश्चकार ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ sa viprārjitayodhanārthastaiḥ kalpitasvastyayano'tha viprānpradakṣiṇīkṛtya kṛtapraṇāmaḥ prahrādamāmantrya namaścakāra
translation: Thus with all the military equipment procured for him by Brāhmaṇas, and having got the Svastyayana (auspicious rites) performed through them, Bali circumambulated them respectfully, paid obeisance to them and taking his leave of Prahlāda, bowed down to him.

devanagari: अथारुह्य रथं दिव्यं भृगुदत्तं महारथः । सुस्रग्धरोऽथ सन्नह्य धन्वी खड्गी धृतेषुधिः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: athāruhya rathaṃ divyaṃ bhṛgudattaṃ mahārathaḥsusragdharo'tha sannahya dhanvī khaḍgī dhṛteṣudhiḥ
translation: Ascending into the celestial chariot conferred upon him by the Bhṛgus (Śukra and his clansmen, by performing the sacrifice), the mighty (chariot) warrior Bali, wearing that excellent wreath, put on his armour, armed himself with the bow, the sword and tied up the (inexhaustible) quivers. With his arms radiant with gold armlets and with dazzling ear-rings (of the shape of an alligator) he shone like the fire blazing in its sacrificial place, when he mounted the chariot.

devanagari: हेमाङ्गदलसद्बाहुः स्फुरन्मकरकुण्डलः । रराज रथमारूढो धिष्ण्यस्थ इव हव्यवाट् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: hemāṅgadalasadbāhuḥ sphuranmakarakuṇḍalaḥrarāja rathamārūḍho dhiṣṇyastha iva havyavāṭ
translation: Ascending into the celestial chariot conferred upon him by the Bhṛgus (Śukra and his clansmen, by performing the sacrifice), the mighty (chariot) warrior Bali, wearing that excellent wreath, put on his armour, armed himself with the bow, the sword and tied up the (inexhaustible) quivers. With his arms radiant with gold armlets and with dazzling ear-rings (of the shape of an alligator) he shone like the fire blazing in its sacrificial place, when he mounted the chariot.

devanagari: तुल्यैश्वर्यबलश्रीभिः स्वयूथैर्दैत्ययूथपैः । पिबद्भिरिव खं दृग्भिर्दहद्भिः परिधीनिव ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tulyaiśvaryabalaśrībhiḥ svayūthairdaityayūthapaiḥpibadbhiriva khaṃ dṛgbhirdahadbhiḥ paridhīniva
translation: Accompanied by his retinue and commanders of (other) Daitya legions who were his equals in command, prowess and splendour, and were (as if) drinking the skies and burning the quarters with (their glaring) eyes, and leading the surrounding mighty Daitya army, he marched against the highly prosperous capital of Indra, shaking as it were the heaven and the earth (by its thumping march).

devanagari: वृतो विकर्षन्महतीमासुरीं ध्वजिनीं विभुः । ययाविन्द्र पुरीं स्वृद्धां कम्पयन्निव रोदसी ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛto vikarṣanmahatīmāsurīṃ dhvajinīṃ vibhuḥyayāvindra purīṃ svṛddhāṃ kampayanniva rodasī
translation: Accompanied by his retinue and commanders of (other) Daitya legions who were his equals in command, prowess and splendour, and were (as if) drinking the skies and burning the quarters with (their glaring) eyes, and leading the surrounding mighty Daitya army, he marched against the highly prosperous capital of Indra, shaking as it were the heaven and the earth (by its thumping march).

devanagari: रम्यामुपवनोद्यानैः श्रीमद्भिर्नन्दनादिभिः । कूजद्विहङ्गमिथुनैर्गायन्मत्तमधुव्रतैः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: ramyāmupavanodyānaiḥ śrīmadbhirnandanādibhiḥkūjadvihaṅgamithunairgāyanmattamadhuvrataiḥ
translation: The city appeared beautiful with its splendid gardens and parks like Nandana and others, resonant with its warbling pairs of birds and humming intoxicated black-bees.

devanagari: प्रवालफलपुष्पोरु भारशाखामरद्रुमैः । हंससारसचक्राह्व कारण्डवकुलाकुलाः । नलिन्यो यत्र क्रीडन्ति प्रमदाः सुरसेविताः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: pravālaphalapuṣporu bhāraśākhāmaradrumaiḥhaṃsasārasacakrāhva kāraṇḍavakulākulāḥnalinyo yatra krīḍanti pramadāḥ surasevitāḥ
translation: (It looked charming) with the celestial trees whose branches were heavily laden with tender foliage, fruits and blossoms of flowers, with lotus-ponds thereof, full of acquatic birds like swans, cranes, ruddy geese (Cakrāhva), and ducks and young celestials, damsels engaged in watersports with gods therein.

devanagari: आकाशगङ्गया देव्या वृतां परिखभूतया । प्राकारेणाग्निवर्णेन साट्टालेनोन्नतेन च ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: ākāśagaṅgayā devyā vṛtāṃ parikhabhūtayāprākāreṇāgnivarṇena sāṭṭālenonnatena ca
translation: It was girt on all sides by the heavenly Gaṅgā (Mandākinī) as its moat. It was surrounded with fortifications of gold, shining like fire and was provided with high towers.

devanagari: रुक्मपट्टकपाटैश्च द्वारैः स्फटिकगोपुरैः । जुष्टां विभक्तप्रपथां विश्वकर्मविनिर्मिताम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: rukmapaṭṭakapāṭaiśca dvāraiḥ sphaṭikagopuraiḥjuṣṭāṃ vibhaktaprapathāṃ viśvakarmavinirmitām
translation: Constructed by Viśvakarmā, the entrance and doors of the houses were fitted with panels of gold while the citygates were of crystals and all the roads were well laid down.

devanagari: सभाचत्वररथ्याढ्यां विमानैर्न्यर्बुदैर्युताम् । शृङ्गाटकैर्मणिमयैर्वज्रविद्रुमवेदिभिः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: sabhācatvararathyāḍhyāṃ vimānairnyarbudairyutāmśṛṅgāṭakairmaṇimayairvajravidrumavedibhiḥ
translation: It appeared beautiful with its assembly halls, quadrangles and streets; it was full of trillions of mansions (or heavenly cars); the quadrangles were paved with precious stones and platforms were studded with diamonds and corals.

devanagari: यत्र नित्यवयोरूपाः श्यामा विरजवाससः । भ्राजन्ते रूपवन्नार्यो ह्यर्चिर्भिरिव वह्नयः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: yatra nityavayorūpāḥ śyāmā virajavāsasaḥbhrājante rūpavannāryo hyarcirbhiriva vahnayaḥ
translation: There young damsels of eternal youth and unfading beauty (ever) appearing sixteen years of age, wearing purest raiment, appeared resplendent like fires ablaze with flames.

devanagari: सुरस्त्रीकेशविभ्रष्ट नवसौगन्धिकस्रजाम् । यत्रामोदमुपादाय मार्ग आवाति मारुतः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: surastrīkeśavibhraṣṭa navasaugandhikasrajāmyatrāmodamupādāya mārga āvāti mārutaḥ
translation: Breezes wafting the fragrance of wreathes of fresh flowers dropped down from the braids of hair of the heavenly damsels blow through its streets.

devanagari: हेमजालाक्षनिर्गच्छद्धूमेनागुरुगन्धिना । पाण्डुरेण प्रतिच्छन्न मार्गे यान्ति सुरप्रियाः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: hemajālākṣanirgacchaddhūmenāgurugandhināpāṇḍureṇa praticchanna mārge yānti surapriyāḥ
translation: The beloveds of gods (Heavenly nymphs) pace through the streets, covered with whitish fragrant fumes of the aguru (a kind of sandal-wood) that escape through the gold- windows (of houses).

devanagari: मुक्तावितानैर्मणिहेमकेतुभिर्नानापताकावलभीभिरावृताम् । शिखण्डिपारावतभृङ्गनादितां वैमानिकस्त्रीकलगीतमङ्गलाम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: muktāvitānairmaṇihemaketubhirnānāpatākāvalabhībhirāvṛtāmśikhaṇḍipārāvatabhṛṅganāditāṃ vaimānikastrīkalagītamaṅgalām
translation: It was covered with pearl-canopies and flag-staffs studded with precious stones, and balconies decorated all around with buntings. It was resonant with notes of peacocks, pigeons, hummings of black-bees and appeared gay and auspicious by the sweet songs of celestial damsels.

devanagari: मृदङ्गशङ्खानकदुन्दुभिस्वनैः सतालवीणामुरजेष्टवेणुभिः । नृत्यैः सवाद्यैरुपदेवगीतकैर्मनोरमां स्वप्रभया जितप्रभाम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: mṛdaṅgaśaṅkhānakadundubhisvanaiḥ satālavīṇāmurajeṣṭaveṇubhiḥnṛtyaiḥ savādyairupadevagītakairmanoramāṃ svaprabhayā jitaprabhām
translation: It was pleasing to the mind with its sounds of mṛdaṅga, conchs, drums and kettle-drums, as also with the sweet rhythmic sound of lutes, murajas, ṛṣṭis (kind of musical instruments) and flutes regulated to the beat of the cymbals, with dances accompanied with orchestral music and songs of demigods (such as Gandharvas, Kinnaras) and had surpassed with its effulgence, the presiding deity of lustre.[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
v.l. The presiding deities of the sun and other planets.

devanagari: यां न व्रजन्त्यधर्मिष्ठाः खला भूतद्रुहः शठाः । मानिनः कामिनो लुब्धा एभिर्हीना व्रजन्ति यत् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: yāṃ na vrajantyadharmiṣṭhāḥ khalā bhūtadruhaḥ śaṭhāḥmāninaḥ kāmino lubdhā ebhirhīnā vrajanti yat
translation: The impious, wicked, the cruel to other beings, the rogues, the proud, the lustful and the avaricious have no access to it; only those who are free from these defects can reach it.

devanagari: तां देवधानीं स वरूथिनीपतिर्बहिः समन्ताद्रुरुधे पृतन्यया । आचार्यदत्तं जलजं महास्वनं दध्मौ प्रयुञ्जन्भयमिन्द्रयोषिताम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃ devadhānīṃ sa varūthinīpatirbahiḥ samantādrurudhe pṛtanyayāācāryadattaṃ jalajaṃ mahāsvanaṃ dadhmau prayuñjanbhayamindrayoṣitām
translation: (Bali) the commander-in-chief of that army, laid siege to the capital of gods with his forces and blew his tremendously sounding conch conferred on him by his preceptor, striking terror in the hearts of Indra’s women.

devanagari: मघवांस्तमभिप्रेत्य बलेः परममुद्यमम् । सर्वदेवगणोपेतो गुरुमेतदुवाच ह ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: maghavāṃstamabhipretya baleḥ paramamudyamamsarvadevagaṇopeto gurumetaduvāca ha
translation: Comprehending the full implications of the supreme efforts of Bali, Indra, accompanied by all the legions of gods (approached) his preceptor (Bṛhaspati) and spoke to him as follows:

devanagari: भगवन्नुद्यमो भूयान्बलेर्नः पूर्ववैरिणः । अविषह्यमिमं मन्ये केनासीत्तेजसोर्जितः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavannudyamo bhūyānbalernaḥ pūrvavairiṇaḥaviṣahyamimaṃ manye kenāsīttejasorjitaḥ
translation: “Worshipful Sir! Tremendous are the (military) preparations of Bali, our old arch-enemy. Me-thinks, they are (now) irresistible. By what energy has he become so overwhelmingly powerful?

devanagari: नैनं कश्चित्कुतो वापि प्रतिव्योढुमधीश्वरः । पिबन्निव मुखेनेदं लिहन्निव दिशो दश । दहन्निव दिशो दृग्भिः संवर्ताग्निरिवोत्थितः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: nainaṃ kaścitkuto vāpi prativyoḍhumadhīśvaraḥpibanniva mukhenedaṃ lihanniva diśo daśadahanniva diśo dṛgbhiḥ saṃvartāgnirivotthitaḥ
translation: None is able by any means whatever to approach him or withstand him. He is swallowing the world with his mouth, licking (as it were) the ten quarters, and burning them down by his fiery eyes. He has emerged against us like the fire of dissolution consuming the universe.

devanagari: ब्रूहि कारणमेतस्य दुर्धर्षत्वस्य मद्रिपोः । ओजः सहो बलं तेजो यत एतत्समुद्यमः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: brūhi kāraṇametasya durdharṣatvasya madripoḥojaḥ saho balaṃ tejo yata etatsamudyamaḥ
translation: Be pleased to disclose to me the cause of the irresistibleness of my foe, whereby he derived this energy, mental courage and physical power and prowess and was enabled to make such tremendous efforts.

devanagari: श्रीगुरुरुवाच । जानामि मघवञ्छत्रोरुन्नतेरस्य कारणम् । शिष्यायोपभृतं तेजो भृगुभिर्ब्रह्मवादिभिः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīgururuvācajānāmi maghavañchatrorunnaterasya kāraṇamśiṣyāyopabhṛtaṃ tejo bhṛgubhirbrahmavādibhiḥ
translation:
The Preceptor (Bṛhaspati) Explained:
“I know, Oh Indra, the cause of this growth of power of your enemy. It is due to the infusion of spiritual energy in their disciple by those experts in Vedic lore, the Bhṛgus (in exchange of the surrender of all his property to them).

devanagari: ओजस्विनं बलिं जेतुं न समर्थोऽस्ति कश्चन । भवद्विधो भवान्वापि वर्जयित्वेश्वरं हरिम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: ojasvinaṃ baliṃ jetuṃ na samartho'sti kaścanabhavadvidho bhavānvāpi varjayitveśvaraṃ harim
translation: With the exception of the Almighty Ruler Hari neither your compeer nor you can stand before him (for confrontation) like human beings before the god of death.

devanagari: विजेष्यति न कोऽप्येनं ब्रह्मतेजःसमेधितम् । नास्य शक्तः पुरः स्थातुं कृतान्तस्य यथा जनाः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: vijeṣyati na ko'pyenaṃ brahmatejaḥsamedhitamnāsya śaktaḥ puraḥ sthātuṃ kṛtāntasya yathā janāḥ
translation: Therefore, all of you should now quit the celestial region and go in hiding, biding your favourable time which would bring in the decline of your enemy.

devanagari: तस्मान्निलयमुत्सृज्य यूयं सर्वे त्रिविष्टपम् । यात कालं प्रतीक्षन्तो यतः शत्रोर्विपर्ययः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmānnilayamutsṛjya yūyaṃ sarve triviṣṭapamyāta kālaṃ pratīkṣanto yataḥ śatrorviparyayaḥ
translation: With the spiritual power of the Brāhmaṇas, he is getting stronger still and is now of irresistible prowess. (In due course) by insulting those very Brāhmaṇas, he will meet destruction along with his kith and kin.”

devanagari: एष विप्रबलोदर्कः सम्प्रत्यूर्जितविक्रमः । तेषामेवापमानेन सानुबन्धो विनङ्क्ष्यति ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa viprabalodarkaḥ sampratyūrjitavikramaḥteṣāmevāpamānena sānubandho vinaṅkṣyati
translation: Having thus received a wise counsel after mature deliberation from their preceptor who viewed (the reality of the situation in the proper perspective) gods, being capable of assuming any form they like, vacated the celestial region.

devanagari: एवं सुमन्त्रितार्थास्ते गुरुणार्थानुदर्शिना । हित्वा त्रिविष्टपं जग्मुर्गीर्वाणाः कामरूपिणः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ sumantritārthāste guruṇārthānudarśināhitvā triviṣṭapaṃ jagmurgīrvāṇāḥ kāmarūpiṇaḥ
translation: When the gods disappeared, Bali, the son of Virocana established himself in the capital of gods and brought under control all the three worlds.

devanagari: देवेष्वथ निलीनेषु बलिर्वैरोचनः पुरीम् । देवधानीमधिष्ठाय वशं निन्ये जगत्त्रयम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: deveṣvatha nilīneṣu balirvairocanaḥ purīmdevadhānīmadhiṣṭhāya vaśaṃ ninye jagattrayam
translation: Bhṛgus who were affectionate to their disciple who had (now) conquered the universe, and who was devoted to them, made him perform one hundred horse sacrifices (to stabilise his Indra-hood).

devanagari: तं विश्वजयिनं शिष्यं भृगवः शिष्यवत्सलाः । शतेन हयमेधानामनुव्रतमयाजयन् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ viśvajayinaṃ śiṣyaṃ bhṛgavaḥ śiṣyavatsalāḥśatena hayamedhānāmanuvratamayājayan
translation: By virtue of his greatness in the performance of one hundred horse-sacrifices he diffused his reputation, already known all over the three worlds, in all quarters and shone like the moon, the lord of stars.

devanagari: ततस्तदनुभावेन भुवनत्रयविश्रुताम् । कीर्तिं दिक्षु वितन्वानः स रेज उडुराडिव ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: tatastadanubhāvena bhuvanatrayaviśrutāmkīrtiṃ dikṣu vitanvānaḥ sa reja uḍurāḍiva
translation:
Considering himself as one who had accomplished all his desires, the noble-minded Bali enjoyed the highly prosperous royal fortune blessed upon him by Brāhmaṇas.
[back to top]

devanagari: बुभुजे च श्रियं स्वृद्धां द्विजदेवोपलम्भिताम् । कृतकृत्यमिवात्मानं मन्यमानो महामनाः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: bubhuje ca śriyaṃ svṛddhāṃ dvijadevopalambhitāmkṛtakṛtyamivātmānaṃ manyamāno mahāmanāḥ
